name: Pocatello (United States)

colors.symbolism: the sky, and the blue line of the Portneuf River near the base
the three mountain peaks; also industry, recreation, and education
the compass rose — the region's agricultural ties and the prosperity of the city
the lower portion of the compass star, suggesting the snowy mountain peaks and forming an abstract arrowhead for the area's Native American history

description: A medium-blue field bearing three overlapping red mountain peaks that rise from a red band along the bottom — the largest at the hoist, the two others stepping down toward the fly. Above the tallest peak is a gold eight-pointed compass rose whose lower point is white, and a thin blue line crosses the red band near the base.
history: Adopted on 20 July 2017, the 'Mountains Left' design replaced an unofficial 2001-2017 flag (the 'Proud to Be Pocatello' Chamber of Commerce logo) that a 2004 North American Vexillological Association survey ranked last of 150 U.S. city flags. Roman Mars's 2015 TED talk about the flag prompted a public redesign contest that drew 709 submissions from 26 countries; the winning flag combines elements of several entries.

features.symbolism: the blue field represents the sky

features.role: three overlapping peaks — Scout Mountain, Kinport Peak, and Chinese Peak — largest at the hoist, rising from a red band along the base
features.symbolism: the mountains of the nearby Bannock and Portneuf ranges; the three peaks also symbolise industry, recreation, and education

features.role: eight-pointed compass-rose star above the largest peak, its lower point white
features.symbolism: a compass rose marking Pocatello as a transportation and trade hub for rail, road, and air, and pointing to the city's past, present, and future; the white lower portion suggests the snowy peaks and forms an abstract arrowhead acknowledging the area's Native American history

features.role: thin blue line across the red band near the base
